title: OpenAI agrees to stagger rollout of its most powerful model to only Trump-approved customers
description: It is the second time in a month that a frontier lab’s most powerful model has been held back from general release over fears about cyber capabilities.
content:
OpenAI is staggering the rollout of its newest and most powerful AI model after a request from the Trump administration. To get access to the new model, customers must first be cleared by the U.S. government, the company said on Friday.



The model, called GPT-5.6 Sol, is the flagship in a new tier of more advanced models that includes a more efficient model called Terra and its cheaper cousin Luna. OpenAI says that Sol is its strongest model yet, able to complete 50% of long-running professional tasks and tops all previous OpenAI models on coding capabilities. OpenAI said it hopes to make all three generally available in the coming weeks.



The Information first reported that the Trump administration asked OpenAI to stagger release of the new model over security concerns.



The move represents a broader shift in how the U.S. government is approaching frontier AI. Advanced cyber capabilities displayed by Anthropic’s Mythos and OpenAI’s GPT-cyber have caused concern in Washington. By limiting access the government is attempting to ensure that those capabilities don’t end up in the hands of bad actors or hostile nation-states.



It is also the second time in a month that a frontier lab’s best model has been held back from general release over capability concerns. In early June, the Commerce Department issued export controls on Anthropic that forced the lab to cut off foreign access to two of its top models, citing national security concerns. Anthropic disputed the order, but was left with no choice but to pull the models offline.



Earlier this month, Trump also signed an executive order directing federal agencies to establish a framework under which AI companies could voluntarily provide the government with early access to powerful new models for up to 30 days before broader release. 



OpenAI describes its own situation as voluntary, in contrast to Anthropic’s situation.



“As part of our ongoing engagement with the U.S. government, we previewed our plans and the models’ capabilities ahead of today’s launch. At their request, we are starting with a limited preview for a small group of trusted partners whose participation has been shared with the government,” the company said in a blog post. 



However, the company also said it was not in favor of this kind of government access process becoming the “long-term default.”



“We are taking this short-term step because we believe it is the strongest path to broader availability in the coming weeks,” the company wrote, adding that it was working with the administration to develop a cyber executive order framework and a “repeatable process for future model releases.”



Capability concerns



OpenAI emphasized that Sol made its strongest gains in cybersecurity, specifically vulnerability and exploitation. There will be two new modes: “max” and “ultra,” which will allow the model to reason longer and coordinate agents for specific tasks. On a key cybersecurity benchmark, OpenAI previously said the model was “competitive with” Anthropic’s Mythos. GPT-5.6 Sol uses approximately one-third of the tokens used by Mythos but appears to lag slightly behind Mythos 5, a slightly more capable model from Anthropic.



OpenAI is pairing the release with what it calls its most extensive safeguards to date, and says that the model preview will police its own use. For higher-risk cases, the company says a larger model will review the conversation and could withhold a response if it’s judged to violate policy. 



It said that, despite the government gating, Sol did not cross the “Cyber Critical” threshold in its “Preparedness Framework”: In tests with Firefox and Chrome, it found the seeds of an exploit but did not produce a working one. OpenAI said it had spent 700,000 GPU hours hacking itself to try to identify vulnerabilities, and humans will conduct two more weeks of the tests before launch.



The limited rollout is a transitional period, and linked to President Trump’s June 2 executive order that directed agencies to build a framework for vetting models before release, according to OpenAI. Since that framework doesn’t exist yet, OpenAI says it conducted a phased rollout at the government’s request. 



The initial users are customers who have been approved by the U.S. government, with the list expanding next week, according to OpenAI. The company said that the process looks like OpenAI sharing names and the government giving feedback. 



Sol is priced at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output tokens, compared with Terra at $2.50 and $15, respectively, and Luna at $1 and $6. 



An improvised licensing regime 



The recent steps toward any kind of attempt to regulate AI also represents a striking reversal for an administration that, on its first day in office, had rescinded a Biden-era requirement for AI companies to submit safety tests to the government, calling it overly burdensome.



However, critics have argued that, by pursuing an ad hoc approach to containing the risks, what is emerging looks less like a coherent regulatory system and more like an improvised licensing regime. Jonathan Iwry, a fellow at the Wharton Accountable AI Lab, previously told Fortune that the government is “repurposing existing legal authorities into what is effectively a backdoor licensing regime.”



Dean Ball, a former Trump administration AI advisor who has since become a vocal critic of its recent decisions, argued that since Mythos, the United States has had an “informal” licensing regime for AI, “with no consistent rules or firm boundaries on state power or public transparency.”



Critics warn that an informal system, with no published criteria or appeal process, opens the door to discrimination—giving the government unchecked power to decide which companies get access to the market and which do not, with no legal recourse for those on the wrong side of that decision.
This story was originally featured on Fortune.com

title: Anthropic and OpenAI waged a $27 million proxy war in a Manhattan congressional race. The winner told them both to get lost
description: Micah Lasher won the most expensive AI election yet—then used his victory speech to reject both companies and promise to regulate them anyway.
content:
Micah Lasher had a message for the two AI companies that had just spent $27 million trying to decide who would represent Manhattan in Congress.



“I have some news for the two big AI companies who’ve taken such an unusual interest in who won this congressional seat,” he said from the podium Tuesday night. “I won’t be taking my cues from either of you when it comes to protecting our kids, our jobs, our environment.”



Lasher—who no AI company spent big money to elect—had just beaten Alex Bores, a state assemblyman who became the unlikely center of an extraordinary bidding war between rival AI factions. Pro-safety AI super PACs, including Public First Action backed by Anthropic, poured $19 million into supporting Bores. Leading the Future, tied to OpenAI president Greg Brockman and Andreessen Horowitz, spent $8 million trying to destroy him. Lasher won with 39% of the vote (while Kennedy scion Jack Schlossberg came in a distant third place). And then Lasher pledged to pursue the same AI regulation agenda as the man he beat.



Not only did Leading the Future fail to prevent the election of the pro-AI regulation, but Public First Action fell short of its goal of bragging rights, according to Adam Kovacevich, a former Google public policy executive and founder of Chamber of Progress, a left-of-center technology trade group.



“They wanted a world in which they could say they elected a vocal AI regulation champion,” Kovacevich told Fortune.



New York’s primary was exceptional, he said. It’s the only election in the country thus far where both sides of the AI world—those in favor of widespread regulation like Anthropic versus those favoring more leeway for innovation like OpenAI—battled over the same race. The result sheds light on a national pattern: Across 35 elections and millions in spending, AI companies are getting very little in return.



“It’s still pretty early to call any of these strategies a complete success or complete failure,” Kovacevich said.



The new AI battlefront



AI-related super PACs have spent more than $50 million on 2026 elections, including $22 million from “pro-innovation” groups like Leading the Future and nearly $28 million from “pro-safety” PACs like Public First Action, according to Transformer, a platformer that aggregates Federal Election Commission filings. 



The trend of AI companies throwing their hats into U.S. elections began in earnest in 2024, when Senator Scott Wiener (D-San Francisco) authored Senate Bill 1047, the first state-level regulatory guidelines for advanced AI. Athropic put its tentative support behind an amended version of the billion in a letter to Gov. Gavin Newsom, who ultimately vetoes the bill over concerns of it stifling innovation and being too broad in scope. The tech company, however, went on to endorse additional California legislation regulating AI, adopting the strategy of supporting a patchwork of statewide efforts to place guardrails on the technology. 



The following year in August 2025, Andreessen Horowitz, Brockman, and Perplexity, among other investors, launched Leading the Future with more than $100 million in initial funding. Kovacevich argued the PAC’s creation was in direct response to Anthropic’s increased involvement in AI-related legislation—and a continuation of Anthropic and OpenAI’s opposing ethoses surrounding AI regulation. (Anthropic cofounders Dario and Daniela Amodei split off from OpenAI in early 2021 over AI safety disagreements to form Anthropic.)



“Absent anthropic trying to advance those laws,” Kovacevich said, “I don’t think any of this would have happened necessarily.”



PACs are looking for politicians, not voters



But AI companies throwing support behind candidates may have less to do with pushing legislation than making an example for other politicians, Kovacevich noted: From Leading the Future’s perspective, if you lead a push for AI regulation without the industry’s input, you will be targeted. Conversely, from Public First Action’s point of view, if you lead the push for AI regulation, you can count on its political support.



“A lot of these PAC strategies are partly about helping your friends and hurting your opponents, but they are partly about sending a signal to other politicians,” he said.



He suggested that at this juncture, tech companies may have little influence on actual legislation. Democrats are poised to take over the Congress, when they will likely work to pass bipartisan legislation regulating AI anyways.



It’s hard to say if PAC spending is the make-or-break force behind any election, and AI-related PACs are no exception. Lasher was expected to beat Bores and also received support in the form of a $10 million contribution from former New York City Mayor Michael Bloomberg. But the New York race was exceptional because in most cases, Kovacevich explained, AI-related PACs support the incumbent, who overwhelmingly win elections over new entrants.



American voters aren’t ready to care about AI



Another reason these PACs’ influence may be limited at this point is because thus far, AI has failed to make meaningful impressions during elections.



“At the end of the day, I just am not convinced that AI is a top priority for voters,” Kovacevich said.



While AI remains unpopular—an NBC News poll from March found 57% of voters believe its risks outweigh its benefits—it’s not top-of-mind during election season. David Shor’s Blue Rose Research found among more than 6,000 respondents that while AI was the issue that saw the biggest leap in importance for U.S. voters, it still ranked No. 29 among 39 issues the research firm measured.



The strategic picture remains unsettled for both sides. A slow-moving Congress favors Leading the Future; a state-by-state regulatory patchwork favors Anthropic and Public First Action. Neither scenario was resolved on Tuesday.



What NY-12 made clear is how unlikely a repeat becomes. “It’s hard to see another battle like this particular one on the horizon,” Kovacevich said.




This story was originally featured on Fortune.com

title: Nehal Mota champions a holistic approach to financial fitness through Finnovate
description: As conversations around personal finance increasingly move beyond investments and returns, a growing number of financial professionals are advocating for a more comprehensive approach to managing money. Among them is Nehal Mota, Co-Founder of Finnovate, who has spent nearly two decades promoting financial fitness as a long-term discipline rather than a short-term pursuit of wealth creation.Founded in 2007 by Nehal Mota and Naveen Singh, Finnovate was established with the objective of helping individuals and families make informed financial decisions through structured planning. Over the years, the firm has worked with thousands of families across India, focusing on goal-based financial strategies designed to align with different life stages and aspirations.Mota's approach stems from the belief that financial well-being cannot be measured solely through investment performance. Instead, she advocates a broader framework that encompasses multiple aspects of personal finance, including cash flow management, risk protection, wealth creation, retirement preparedness, estate planning, and financial goal setting.This philosophy led to the development of a financial fitness model that seeks to bring clarity and discipline to the way people manage their finances. Rather than emphasizing products, the model focuses on creating long-term financial habits and decision-making frameworks that can help individuals navigate changing economic circumstances.Under Mota's leadership, Finnovate adopted a hybrid advisory structure that combines technology with human expertise. The model aims to provide personalized financial guidance that is structured and purposeful, while maintaining transparency and consistency throughout the planning process.According to Mota, financial planning should remain unbiased and aligned with the interests of clients. “My main objective with Finnovate was to build a fiduciary advisory model that remains unbiased, product-agnostic, and deeply personalized. We believe financial planning should empower families to make confident decisions and unlock their true financial potential,” she said.As financial literacy continues to gain prominence across India, Mota remains focused on encouraging individuals to view financial planning as an ongoing journey rather than a one-time activity. Her work reflects a growing shift within the industry toward holistic financial wellness, where long-term security and life goals take precedence over short-term market outcomes.The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: FCC accused of hiding Chairman Carr's messages with DOGE and Musk
description: FCC refuses to provide messages, has "wasted a year" of court's time, filing says.
content:
An advocacy group trying to investigate DOGE's influence on the Federal Communications Commission accused the FCC of failing to comply with a public records request and of concealing Chairman Brendan Carr's use of the Signal messaging service.
"The evidence clearly demonstrates that the FCC has acted in bad faith by withholding documents responsive to Plaintiffs’ FOIA [Freedom of Information Act] request," journalist Nina Burleigh and advocacy group Frequency Forward said in a filing yesterday in US District Court for the District of Columbia. "The FCC acted in bad faith when it redefined the search criteria without notice to Plaintiffs or this Court. Further, the FCC acted in bad faith by concealing the fact that the Chairman Carr has a Signal account on a phone he uses to conduct government business."
Burleigh and Frequency Forward sued the FCC last year, alleging that it violated the Freedom of Information Act by wrongfully withholding agency records. In August 2025, a federal judge ordered the FCC to produce documents and criticized it for a “vague and uninformative” response to the lawsuit.Read full article

title: IND vs IRE 1st T20I: Shreyas Iyer's captaincy stint starts with a humiliating 34-run loss to Ireland
description: Shreyas Iyer's captaincy stint starts on a disappointing note as India suffered a humbling 34-run loss to Ireland in the first T20I of the 3-match series.
content: Ireland inflicted a 34-run loss on India in the first T20I of the 3-match series, held at Stormont in Belfast on Friday. Batting first, Ireland posted a challenging total of 182/9 in 20 overs. Lorcan Tucker emerged as the standout performer with the bat with a 50-run knock. In reply, India bundled out at 148 in 18.5 overs. Matthew Hollard was the most successful with the ball as he picked up three wickets and gave away just 28 runs in his four overs. This result will go down in history as this was the first time Ireland have beaten India in any format of the sport.Immaculate Ireland bowlers gnawed on Indian batters' impatience on a rather pacy pitch with exemplary lengths, etching a historic 34-run victory in the first T20I match here on Friday.It was Ireland's first-ever victory over reigning T20 world champions India in any format, and the hosts have now taken a 1-0 lead in the two-match series.Chasing 183 on a pitch that had enough assistance for quick bowlers was not improbable but needed a different, pragmatic approach after power play.(With agency inputs)

title: Kylian Mbappe vs Erling Haaland: Stats, head-to-head and net worth comparison ahead of France vs Norway FIFA World Cup 2026 game
description: Mbappe vs Haaland: Ahead of the start of the tonight's France vs Norway encounter in the FIFA World Cup 2026, let's take a look at overall comparison between two of the biggest stars of football, Kylian Mbappe of France and Erling Haaland of Norway.
content: Mbappe vs Haaland: With the Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi era at its twilight, Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland have emerged as the two biggest names who are currently ruling the landscape of football. Both these gladiators will come across each other tonight in the grandest spectacle of the game, i.e., the FIFA World Cup 2026. Both players have lived up to their names in the tournament so far, netting four goals apiece. However, when the lights are at their brightest, who will emerge as the victor?Ahead of the start of the tonight's game, let's take a look at different aspects and keep Mbappe and Haaland under each lens.The Disgrace of Gijón: How the controversial 1982 Germany vs Austria World Cup match prompted FIFA to change rules of the game Mbappe vs Haaland: Career stats and goal recordsWhile both are prolific goal scorers, they have mastered various facets that have led them to success. About Mbappe, the Frenchman is arguably the fastest player in the sport currently, has towering dribbling ability and versatility. Haaland, on the other hand, is known for his physical presence, movement inside the box and remarkable finishing ability.When it comes to overall career numbers, Mbappe, 27, has scored more goals. He has amassed a total of 420 goals in 570 appearances for club and country. Notably, Mbappe has also registered more than 150 assists, a characteristic of his game which is not talked about often. Haaland, despite playing fewer games, has crossed the 350-goal mark and has more than 60 assists to his name.At the club level, Mbappe established himself at Paris Saint-Germain before moving to Real Madrid, where he has continued his impressive run. Haaland has enjoyed exquisite spells at Borussia Dortmund and Manchester City, breaking several Premier League scoring records along the way.In the UEFA Champions League, both have outstanding records. Mbappe has scored around 70 goals in nearly 100 appearances, while Haaland has netted more than 50 goals in fewer than 60 matches, highlighting his extraordinary strike rate.How rich is FIFA in comparison to the BCCI and other international sporting bodies like the ICC and the IOC? Mbappe vs Haaland: Head-to-head Kylian Mbappe holds a head-to-head record of 3 wins and 1 loss against Erling Haaland across all UEFA Champions League meetings.Mbappe vs Haaland: Net worth comparisonOff the pitch, Mbappe is among the highest-paid athletes in the world. His estimated net worth is around $250 million, boosted by his Real Madrid contract and endorsement deals with major global brands. Haaland's net worth is expected to be between $80 million and $100 million, backed by his Manchester City salary and growing commercial partnerships. Haaland also has a YouTube channel, and his videos have racked up millions of views. However, it cannot be asserted how much he rakes in from content creation.

title: 20‑Page SIT Report, 10 Big Revelations in Ram Mandir Donation Theft Case
description: The Special Investigation Team (SIT) in Uttar Pradesh has released a report detailing the investigation into the alleged theft of donation funds for the Ram Temple.
content: The Special Investigation Team (SIT), that was constituted by the Uttar Pradesh government on the recommendation of the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Trust to probe the alleged theft of the donation funds at the Ram Temple in Ayodhya, has submitted a 20-page report in the matter. The probing agency in its report talks about how CCTV footage revealed multiple incidents of funds theft, for how long it was going on, how many people are suspected to be involved, and how these acts were being executed.In its 20-page report, the SIT reportedly mentions about 70 theft-related incidents after analyzing the CCTV footage. It mentions that the alleged embezzlement of the donation funds had been going on for 2-3 years.The SIT says that they searched and reviewed CCTV footage from April 27, 2026, to June 5, 2026, and found out that around 70 people are suspected to be involved in the case. The report confirms donation theft based on the evidence found in the investigation so far. The SIT has found lapses in the processes involved from the counting centers of the donation funds and up to its management.The probing agency has charged eight individuals based on electronic evidence, witness interviews, and statements from the accused. It states that the trust had suspected the theft long ago.It also mentions that a SOP was established in a meeting between the Trust and the bank in September 2024 and February 2025. Govind Mishra, representing the State Bank, and Anil Mishra, representing the Trust, signed the document. However, the SOP was not subsequently used in the calculation process, and deliberate negligence was committed.Based on the findings so far, the role of Champat Rai and Anil Mishra has been questioned.Further, during the interrogation, two accused including Tinnu Yadav and Subhash Srivastava, have reportedly made several important revelations related to the entire theft network. The SIT has written in the report that Tinnu had the keys to the bills without permission. According to sources, the names of several bank officials have also emerged during the interrogation as their role is also suspicious in the donation theft case.According to sources, action may also likely be taken against several government employees as the SIT has viewed this case as an organised crime.In its report, the SIT described Tinnu Yadav and Subhash Srivastava as key players in the conspiracy and its execution.The SIT further states in its report that Subhash Srivastava connected others to this network and assumed important responsibilities, such as assigning donation counting duties.The eight arrested are -- Avinash Shukla, Anukalp Mishra, Lav Kush Mishra, Manish Kumar Yadav, Karunesh Pandey, Ram Shankar Mishra, Subhash Srivastava and Ramashankar alias Tinnu Yadav. They were associated with counting cash and valuables received as donations at the temple.      https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=gfuB0j73Mkk8 Accused in Ram Temple Theft Case Arrested, No Clarity on Champat Rai's Resignation  Meanwhile, as investigation advance in the donation theft case, all eight suspects named in the FIR have been arrested and remanded in judicial custody till June 29. Investigators so far have reportedly recovered Rs 79.85 lakh.Amid rumours that Champat Rai, a key figure in controversy, has resigned as the general secretary of the Shree Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust, the Vishva Hindu Parishad (VHP) said it was not aware of any such move. Rai is also the vice president of the VHP.CM Yogi Reassures 'No One' Would Be SparedChief Minister Yogi Adityanath vowed that "no one" found guilty or playing with people's faith would be spared, even as AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal called the case a "dharma yudh", urged a social boycott of the alleged "chanda chors" and claimed Hindus' sentiments had been hurt.      https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KfliqddT_EgAkhilesh's Lankakaand Taunt on BJP Samajwadi Party chief Akhilesh Yadav said the BJP's "Lankakaand" had begun from Ayodhya while the Congress sought a Supreme Court-monitored probe and dissolution of the temple trust.With inputs from PTI

title: Netflix now requires every user profile to be tied to unique email address
description: Update began June 15 and will no longer allow you to share your login info.
content:
Recently, my father called me in a panic.
There were just a few minutes until Netflix would start streaming a live MMA event, and he couldn’t get into my account. For a while, he had accessed Netflix as an add-on member with his own profile through my household’s account. That day, however, he was logged out and couldn’t use my login credentials to watch Netflix. Instead, he saw a prompt asking him to “add an email address to your profile” to continue.

      A Reddit user shared this image of the notification that affected profile owners are seeing.
        Credit:
          Scotti_Dev/Reddit/Netflix
      
After some frantic phone troubleshooting and a couple of password resets, we realized that my father had to create his own login to continue using the extra profile I paid for. Although I was able to get him set up in time (for some disappointing bouts), the situation was confusing and inconvenient.Read full article

title: HDFC Bank's legal review finds no evidence supporting former Chairman Atanu Chakraborty's claims
description: HDFC Bank's independent legal review found no evidence in official records or witness interviews to support the concerns raised by former chairman Atanu Chakraborty.
content: HDFC Bank has said an independent legal review ordered after former chairman Atanu Chakraborty's resignation did not find evidence to support the concerns he had raised before stepping down earlier this year.The review was carried out by external law firms over a period of three months. According to the bank, the investigation examined official records, board documents and interviewed senior officials before reaching its conclusions.An independent review examined board recordsThe bank said the legal team reviewed board and committee meeting minutes, agenda papers, internal communications and thousands of documents covering the two years before Chakraborty's resignation.Independent directors, committee chairpersons, Managing Director and CEO Sashidhar Jagdishan, along with several senior executives, were also interviewed during the process.According to the findings shared by the bank, there was nothing in the available records that supported the concerns mentioned by Chakraborty in his resignation statement.No record of formal objectionsThe review also looked into whether Chakraborty had formally raised objections during board meetings or recorded any disagreement with decisions taken by the board.The legal firms concluded that the meeting records did not show any such objections. They also said the minutes were prepared through an established review process that gave directors an opportunity to suggest changes or record their views before approval.The report added that interviews with board members and senior executives did not support the allegations either.Dubai operations also reviewedThe external legal teams also examined issues related to HDFC Bank's Dubai operations after Chakraborty referred to the matter in comments made following his resignation.According to the report, investigators did not find any evidence showing that he had raised concerns before the board regarding the Dubai business or expressed disagreement over decisions linked to those operations while serving as chairman.Former chairman was invited to participateThe bank said the legal firms made repeated attempts to speak with Chakraborty during the review. However, the interview did not take place.After completing the exercise, the firms informed the board that the available documents and witness statements did not support the claims made in the resignation statement.What happens nextThe legal review was commissioned after Chakraborty's resignation earlier this year, when he cited personal values and ethics as the reason for stepping down.HDFC Bank has now placed the findings before its board. The bank has maintained that the independent review found no material in official records to back the concerns raised by its former chairman.

title: আন্তঃৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় ড্ৰাগছ বিৰোধী দিৱস উপলক্ষে ডবকাত সজাগতা সভা...
description:
ডিজিটেল সংবাদ, ডবকাঃ আন্তঃৰাষ্ট্ৰীয় মাদক দ্ৰব্য অপব্যৱহাৰ আৰু অবৈধ সৰবৰাহ বিৰোধী দিৱস উপলক্ষে “নিচামুক্ত ভাৰত সপ্তাহ”ৰ অংশ হিচাপে শুকুৰবাৰে ডবকাৰ হাজী আনফাৰ আলী মহাবিদ্যালয় অডিট’ৰিয়ামত এখন সজাগতা সভা অনুষ্ঠিত হয়। অনুষ্ঠানটোৰ মূল বিষয় আছিল — “নিচামুক্ত ভাৰত অভিযান – বিকশিত ভাৰতৰ পৰিচয়”।
জিলা সমাজ কল্যাণ কাৰ্যালয়, হোজাইৰ উদ্যোগত আৰু হাজী আনফাৰ আলী মহাবিদ্যালয়ৰ লগতে নৰ্থ ইষ্ট ৰুৰেল ৱেলফেয়াৰ ছ’চাইটি, ডবকাৰ সহযোগত আয়োজিত সভাখনত সভাপতিত্ব কৰে মহাবিদ্যালয়খনৰ অধ্যক্ষ ড° মামন আজম বৰভূঞাই। সভাত মুখ্য অতিথি হিচাপে উপস্থিত থাকি হোজাই জিলাৰ সহকাৰী আয়ুক্ত ডা: বিভূতি সন্দিকৈয়ে বৰ্তমান সময়ত সমাজত বৃদ্ধি পোৱা বিভিন্ন ধৰণৰ আসক্তিৰ ওপৰত গুৰুত্ব আৰোপ কৰি বক্তব্য আগবঢ়ায়। 
তেওঁ কয় যে- আজিৰ সময়ত ড্ৰাগছ আসক্তিৰ লগতে ছ’চিয়েল মিডিয়া এডেকচনো এক গুৰুতৰ সামাজিক সমস্যালৈ পৰিণত হৈছে, যিয়ে বিশেষকৈ যুৱ সমাজৰ ওপৰত গভীৰ নেতিবাচক প্ৰভাৱ পেলাইছে। তেওঁ সকলোকে সচেতন হৈ সুস্থ জীৱনশৈলী গঢ়ি তোলা আৰু সকলো ধৰণৰ আসক্তিৰ পৰা আঁতৰি থকাৰ আহ্বান জনায়।
অনুষ্ঠানত উপস্থিত থাকি হোজাই জিলা সমাজ কল্যাণ বিষয়া নীলশ্বৰ ৰায় আৰু হোজাই ডি এছ পি ভাস্বতী চৌধুৰীয়ে মাদকমুক্ত সমাজ গঢ়ি তোলাৰ ক্ষেত্ৰত জনসচেতনতাৰ প্ৰয়োজনীয়তাৰ ওপৰত ভাষণ প্ৰদান কৰে। লগতে বিন্নাকান্দি চিডিপিঅ’ গীতা বংগ্ৰুংপি, উদালি চিডিপিঅ বিভা মালি আৰু যোগীজান চিডিপিঅ চৰলাংটুক সিংনাৰেও নিজৰ বক্তব্য আগবঢ়ায়।
ইয়াৰ উপৰিও হাজী আনফাৰ আলী মহাবিদ্যালয়ৰ অধ্যাপক-অধ্যাপিকা, ছাত্ৰ-ছাত্ৰী, স্থানীয় বিভিন্ন বিদ্যালয়ৰ শিক্ষাৰ্থী আৰু বহু সংখ্যক স্থানীয় লোক উপস্থিত থাকি অনুষ্ঠানটো সফল কৰি তোলে। অনুষ্ঠানত মাদকমুক্ত সমাজ গঢ়াৰ লগতে যুৱ সমাজক সকলো ধৰণৰ আসক্তিৰ পৰা আঁতৰি থাকিবলৈ আহ্বান জনোৱা হয়।

title: Antibiotic "megacluster" discovery provides new strategy to fight superbugs
description: It's "an exciting advance in efforts to restock the antibiotic arsenal."
content:
Antibiotic resistance has loomed over humans since the moment we started using antibiotics. In the 20th century, the drugs downgraded potentially life-threatening bacterial infections to mere inconveniences—a miracle of modern medicine, it seemed. But the drugs aren't really a human invention; we mostly swiped them from microbes, which have been locked in an arms race with each other for centuries. Microbial evolution has crafted both deadly molecules and clever tricks to dodge death as the wee organisms endlessly battle over turf and resources. More than 80 percent of the antibiotics used in clinics today are based on those turf-war weapons, which scientists refer to as "natural products."
For decades, humans mined antibiotic molecules from microbes and tweaked them to develop new drugs, staying ahead of evolution's cunning countermeasures. But in recent times, new natural products have been harder to find, and the pipeline of new antibiotics has slowed to a trickle. Meanwhile, existing antibiotics have been overused, and resistance has mounted to critical levels. Most antibiotics are single bioactive molecules, and some can be thwarted with single mutations. While the current situation is dire, a study in Nature this week reports a compelling discovery that not only points to a potentially new antibiotic regimen, but also an entirely new strategy to once again get ahead in the microbial arms race.
Exciting find
The study, led by biomedical researcher Eric Brown at McMaster University in Ontario, Canada, reports the discovery of a large block of genes—dubbed a "megacluster"—that codes for four molecules that appear to work in concert to derail a single essential metabolic pathway.Read full article

title: The most coveted accessory at Paris Fashion Week was an ice pack
description: As a historic heat wave gripped the French capital, fashion houses fought to keep guests cool with mist machines, chilled towels, parasols and iced Evian.
content:
The most coveted accessory at the Paris Fashion Week shows this week was not a bag, a sneaker or a watch. It was an ice pack.



As a historic heat wave gripped the French capital, fashion houses fought to keep guests cool with mist machines, chilled towels, parasols and iced Evian on silver platters.



It wasn’t enough. Historic venues sweltered, guests were packed in tight, air conditioning was absent or inadequate and water ran short — at one house, organizers weighed serving none at all, having found only plastic bottles to hand out.



That mattered because Paris Fashion Week is not a minor cultural event.



It is one of France’s most visible export machines: six fashion seasons a year, global luxury houses, celebrities, editors, buyers and clients moving through an industry worth billions, often inside aging venues built for a cooler age.



This week raised a harder question: whether Paris should keep staging menswear and haute couture in the height of summer at all if climate change keeps bringing more frequent and intense heat waves.



“I honestly thought I was going to pass out,” said Ben Freeman, a London-based fashion critic from Australia.



Paris neared 41 degrees Celsius (106 Fahrenheit) during a heat wave that pushed France into emergency mode. Large parts of the country were under red alert, and hospitals were told to prepare for more heat-related cases.



Like the dusty Louvre, which cut hours and said its historic building “remains vulnerable and is not sufficiently adapted to climate change,” fashion week exposed a Paris problem as much as a fashion one: how to keep prestige institutions running when the weather no longer fits the building, the calendar or the crowd.



“Paris Fashion Week is the canary in the mine,” Freeman said.



The deeper contradiction was on the runway.



At a Paris Fashion Week Men’s where the industry paid to imagine next summer could barely survive this one, houses cooled the people watching the shows, then dressed their models in unseasonable leather, neoprene, wool and fur.



“The calendar does not make any sense,” acknowledged Dior’s Jonathan Anderson, blaming fractured delivery cycles and a business that bears no relation to the season outside.



Some in the front row suggested that fashion week in the hottest months be scrapped.



“In Paris we don’t have AC everywhere, it’s quite rare,” said Thomas Levy, 24, a fashion student outside one show. “I don’t know how the models did it this week in some of the leather and knit coats.”



The venues couldn’t cope



Pascal Morand, who heads France’s fashion federation, said organizers were following the government’s heat-wave plan.



“We are conscious of the challenges and very attentive to preserving the Fashion Week experience in this context of structural change,” he told The Associated Press.



The cause ran deeper — an industry whose fixed parts, from the buildings to the clothes, were designed for a cooler world and a customer who lives somewhere else.



The response included earlier shows, more water, more mist, more shade.



Fashion had already been warned about heat management. In March, Celine built an okoumé-wood pavilion in the courtyard of the Institut de France for a winter show, packed guests inside and still saw some leave because of the temperature.



Dior shifted its show to 9 a.m. from mid-afternoon, and Rick Owens moved his forward too. Yet inside Dior’s half-renovated mansion, water was scarce, there was no air conditioning, and some guests looked ready pass out.



The strain had already shown at Milan Fashion Week last week. At Thom Browne’s first show there, giant misting fans ran and black umbrellas went out as guests waited out the midday sun.



Runways out of season



The clothes were made not for summer in Paris but for global markets and customers who pass the hottest months in refrigerated air — the malls of the Gulf, the towers of New York and Shanghai. For them, a wool coat in June is not a contradiction. It is just a purchase.



Louis Vuitton presented wetsuits in neoprene, as well as coats in cashmere and fur.



At Saint Laurent, Anthony Vaccarello sent models through cooling clouds of vapor from a Fujiko Nakaya fog sculpture, then ran hot and cold at once: featherweight, unlined tailoring stripped down for the heat, against leather briefs, choker scarves and transparent shoes fogging with the wearer’s sweat.



Issey Miyake’s IM Men gave the clearest practical answer, handing out ice packs at the door, then bamboo-thread fabrics and shadowy prints that moved with the air rather than against it.



Rick Owens made the anxiety literal, sending models through mist in garments with fans whirring inside. One critic called it metaphor for climate catastrophe.



France’s uneasy cooling debate



Air conditioning remains culturally suspect in France — blamed for sore throats, dismissed as wasteful or bad for the planet — even as heat waves turn cooling into a question of public safety.



President Emmanuel Macron’s government leans toward shade, insulation and trees; environmentalists warn that mass cooling would only deepen the emissions driving the heat.



Europe is the fastest-warming continent, but its old cities are short on the cooling a hotter climate demands. From sport to tourism to construction, industries built around fixed calendars and outdoor crowds are being forced to adapt to heat that comes earlier, lasts longer and climbs higher.



The question is how much longer an aging 19th century Paris can host a summer spectacle where guests need ice packs to reach the finale.



___



Colleen Barry in Milan contributed to this report.
This story was originally featured on Fortune.com

title: Peggy Gou taps the “uplifting energy of summer” for infectious single ‘Wo,man’ featuring Ayra Starr
description:
"I hope this record brings fun summer memories to all who love it”
The post Peggy Gou taps the “uplifting energy of summer” for infectious single ‘Wo,man’ featuring Ayra Starr appeared first on NME.
content:
Peggy Gou has teamed up with Ayra Starr on her new summer-ready single ‘Wo,man’ – check it out below.
The South Korean-born, London-based DJ, producer and artist released the track today (June 26) via XL Recordings, marking her latest release since her 2024 debut album ‘I Hear You’.
A typically euphoric summer dancefloor anthem from Gou, ‘Wo,man’ pairs Gou’s restlessly energetic production with a vocal from Nigerian afrobeats artist Starr that includes the earwormy chorus hook of “Adigidang dang dang di dang”.
Check out the video for ‘Wo,man’, directed by Onda, here:

Speaking about the single, Gou said: “When I was working on ‘Wo,man’, I knew I wanted a voice that could match the song’s uplifting energy for summer. The moment I shared it with Ayra, she got it instantly. Bringing our two worlds together felt completely natural, and she brought the perfect balance of sexiness and swag into the record. I hope this record brings fun summer memories to all who love it.”
Starr added: “I love Peggy – she’s such a cool artist. It was so much fun to make this song with her. It felt like such a summer vibe – I hope everyone enjoys it as much as I enjoyed making it!”
The collaboration follows a previous creative link-up between the pair, after Gou remixed Starr’s single ‘Where Do We Go’ earlier this year.

  










View this post on Instagram

























To mark the release of ‘Wo,man’, Gou is also opening a pop-up boutique in London, dubbed ‘The Gou,tique’, today for one day and night only. It is located at Dream Bags Jaguar Shoes on Kingsland Road in east London from 4pm to 9pm, with free entry on a first-come, first-served basis.
Gou will gift 200 mystery items from her personal collection, alongside 100 items from archival Peggy Gou merchandise lines, while DJs from her Gudu Records roster will soundtrack the event.
Gou is set for a busy summer of live appearances, including a headline show at London’s Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich on August 2.
Earlier this year, Gou also remixed Madonna’s ‘I Feel So Free’, the first preview of Madonna’s upcoming album ‘Confessions II’. She added a ‘90s dance-flavoured touch to the track, built around clattering drums and squelchy synths.
Starr, meanwhile, will be playing at Jorja Smith and Tems’ date of All Points East 2026, alongside the likes of Nia Archives, Elmiene and Kwn.
The post Peggy Gou taps the “uplifting energy of summer” for infectious single ‘Wo,man’ featuring Ayra Starr appeared first on NME.

title: The next-generation leaders reshaping business
description: New Delhi [India], June 22: A new wave of entrepreneurs, founders, and executives is transforming industries through innovation, strategic vision, and bold leadership. These next-generation leaders are not only driving business growth but also creating meaningful impact by challenging conventions and embracing change. In this special feature, we spotlight emerging changemakers whose ideas, resilience, and forward-thinking approach are shaping the future of business and inspiring the next era of leadership.Richa Gangani, Founder, Richa’s Fitness LabRicha Gangani, Founder of Richa’s Fitness Lab, is dedicated to helping individuals build healthier lives through practical, personalized wellness solutions. Her approach is rooted in the belief that sustainable health comes from understanding each person’s unique lifestyle, habits, and nutritional needs rather than following one-size-fits-all trends. Through her work, she empowers clients to adopt realistic routines that fit seamlessly into their daily lives, making long-term consistency achievable. Richa advocates a balanced approach to fitness and nutrition, focusing on lasting well-being over quick fixes. By simplifying health and encouraging mindful lifestyle choices, she continues to inspire individuals to achieve meaningful and sustainable transformations.Mr. Chakravarthi Mittapalli, Vice Chairman, Narasaraopeta Engineering CollegeFor over two decades, Narasaraopeta Engineering College (NEC) has shaped technical education in Andhra Pradesh through academic excellence, industry collaboration, innovation and career-focused learning. With a 48-acre campus, autonomous status, NAAC A+ accreditation and NIRF 201-300 ranking band, NEC is advancing AI-led engineering education under Vice Chairman Chakravarthi Mittapalli, a technologist, entrepreneur and education visionary. The institution’s AI & Robotics Centres of Innovation, industry skilling programmes, incubation initiatives, Intel-backed AI Lab, certified training, internships, research exposure, next-generation tools and Microsoft AI Odyssey recognition reflect its focus on building a future-ready youth.Mr. Tejas Chavan, Director, Green SpacesMr. Tejas Chavan is the Director of Green Spaces and a purpose-driven entrepreneur committed to advancing sustainable living and eco-conscious hospitality. Under his leadership, Grape County Eco Resort & Spa has emerged as one of Maharashtra’s leading eco-tourism destinations, guided by the philosophy of “Co-Exist with Nature.” He has spearheaded transformative initiatives including the plantation of over 60,000 native trees, creation of five rainwater-harvested lakes, 100% water recycling systems, and biodiversity restoration projects. A passionate advocate for sustainable development, regenerative farming, rural empowerment, and responsible tourism, Mr. Chavan continues to demonstrate how environmental conservation and economic growth can work together to create lasting value for communities and future generations.Rituu Jai Mehhta is Director – Marketing (South) & Centre Director, Phoenix Mall of AsiaRituu Jai Mehhta is Director – Marketing (South) & Centre Director, Phoenix Mall of Asia , where she leads marketing and destination strategy across more than five million square feet of premium retail developments in Bengaluru, Chennai, and Pune. With over two decades of experience in retail, marketing, and consumer engagement, she has been instrumental in advancing experiential retail by integrating fashion, entertainment, beauty, gastronomy, and community-led experiences into destination ecosystems. Recognized for combining strategic business leadership with innovative marketing, Rituu has received multiple industry honours, including The Times of India's Woman Power Award as well as Karnataka Woman Entrepreneurship Award among others for her contributions to retail and destination marketing.Mr. Karteesh Reddy Madgula, Mr. Lakshmi Narayana G., and Mr. Sharat Ventrapragada, Designated Partners of GHR Lakshmi Urbanblocks Infra LLPDeveloped by Mr. Karteesh Reddy Madgula, Mr. Lakshmi Narayana G., and Mr. Sharat Ventrapragada (Partners of GHR Lakshmi Urbanblocks Infra LLP), The Cascades Neopolis is a landmark ultra-luxury residential project in Kokapet, Hyderabad. Spanning 7.34 acres, the development features five 63-storey towers housing 1,189 residences and 10 triplex penthouses.As India’s first and the world’s largest WELL pre-certified residential project, this international collaboration prioritizes wellness-led, future-ready living. The joint venture solidified its regional realty leadership by securing Neopolis Plot 15, a premium 4.03-acre corner land parcel boasting dual 150-foot road frontages, with a record-setting highest bid of ₹151.25 crore per acre, ensuring immense long-term value.Mr. Rishi Bharadwaj, CEO and Mr. Krishna Pradyumna, CTO of CultNerdsIT SolutionsCultNerds, Co-founded by Mr. Rishi Bharadwaj, CEO and Mr. Krishna Pradyumna, CTO in 2021 and headquartered in Hyderabad, CultNerds IT Solutions is an award-winning digital transformation agency serving 50+ clients across real estate, hospitality, luxury clubs, retail and education. With 65+ professionals, the company blends technology, marketing, data and AI to improve customer acquisition, engagement and business performance. Its in-house products support data analytics, enrichment, customer nurturing, automation and intelligent workflows. Rishi drives a framework-led culture focused on creativity, accountability and outcomes, while Krishna leads technical innovation and scalable product development for thousands of users.Jimmy Mistry, Founder & Chairman, Della TownshipsDesign Futurist, Jimmy Mistry founded Della Townships, one of India’s fastest-growing asset-light real estate integrated township platforms, to redefine how India lives, works, and dreams through design-led, future-ready, theme-based lifestyle cities. From a 30-key glamping resort in Lonavala to a ₹46,480 crore township pipeline spanning 4,288 acres across India, Jimmy Mistry believes "We become the new Uber or the new Airbnb of the real estate industry. We are an asset-light real estate model." Removing the two biggest barriers to real estate expansion, Della's asset-light model eliminates the need for land ownership and direct regulatory approvals. Instead, the company partners with landowners and developers, focusing on what it does best—conceptualisation, design, development, marketing, and operations through its proprietary CDDMO model.Piyush Jhunjhunwala, Founder & CEO, StockifyPiyush Jhunjhunwala is a seasoned finance professional, Chartered Accountant (India), and Certified Public Accountant (USA) with over 20 years of experience across global markets. Before founding Stockify, he held senior leadership roles at PepsiCo, Reckitt, and Coty, building expertise in finance, strategy, governance, and business operations across India and international markets. As Founder and CEO, Piyush has led Stockify’s mission to democratize access to India’s pre-IPO and unlisted shares market. Under his leadership, the company has expanded into alternative investments with the launch of its Alternative Investment Fund (AIF), evolving into a comprehensive WealthTech platform. He is now focused on broadening investor access to global private market opportunities, including U.S. private equity investments, helping drive long-term wealth creation and portfolio diversification.Dr. Eadhu Krishna K, Managing Director & Chief Consultant, SKCCR Care, Ernakulam, Kerala.Dr. Eadhu Krishna K is a healthcare entrepreneur and the Managing Director & Chief Consultant of SKCCR Care – Ernakulam, Kerala. Building on the institution’s 46-year legacy, he began training under his father at 18 and has since accumulated over 14 years of clinical research and experience in integrative cancer care. He has treated thousands of patients through evidence-based homoeopathic protocols and was honored with the Leading Innovator in Homoeopathic Cancer Care award by the Health Minister of Kerala. At 25, he founded Dr.BOZ Pharma, a company developing plant-based nutraceuticals and homoeopathic formulations for chronic lifestyle diseases, combining clinical expertise with pharmaceutical innovation.Jesicaa Siingh, Founder & CEO, Stanley CommunicationsJesicaa Siingh is the Founder & CEO of Stanley Communications, a global PR agency specializing in luxury, fashion, beauty, hospitality, and culture. As an entrepreneur, she has built a 135+ member women-led team while expanding the agency’s presence to Dubai. Driven by a passion for storytelling and brand building, she has helped shape the India narratives of global brands including Valentino, Bvlgari, Messika, Amiri, Dyson, Versace, SK-II, and Galeries Lafayette.A champion of women in leadership, Jesicaa has built a culture of creativity and accountability, earning recognitions including Times 40 Under 40 and TV9 Network SHE Star Honours.Together, these next-generation leaders represent the evolving face of business—bold, innovative, and impact-driven. Through their vision, adaptability, and determination, they are not only transforming their respective industries but also setting the benchmark for the future of leadership and enterprise.The pages slugged ‘Brand Connect’ are equivalent to advertisements and are not written and produced by Forbes India journalists.

title: Before Jab We Met, Rockstar and Main Vaapas Aaunga, Imtiaz Ali made a love story that almost everyone missed and deserves a second watch
description: As Main Vaapas Aaunga wins praise, fans are rediscovering Imtiaz Ali's overlooked debut romance, a heartfelt film that quietly laid the foundation for his signature style of storytelling.
content: As Main Vaapas Aaunga continues to win hearts and remind audiences why Imtiaz Ali remains one of Hindi cinema's most distinctive storytelling voices, a particular conversation has been circling among film enthusiasts online. Everyone is pointing back to Jab We Met, to Tamasha, to Highway, to Rockstar. But there is a film that predates all of them, a quiet, tender little romantic comedy made in 2005 that most people have either forgotten or never quite discovered, and it is the one that started everything.It was called Socha Na Tha. And it is exactly as sweet as the title suggests.A Debut That Nobody Quite Knew Was ComingIn 2005, Imtiaz Ali was nobody's idea of an upcoming filmmaker. He had spent years working in television, writing and directing for the small screen, largely invisible to the Hindi film industry's power structures. He had a story he wanted to tell, a simple, unassuming romance about two people who meet through an arranged marriage setup, decide they are not right for each other, walk away, and then gradually discover that they have left something important behind.The story found its lead in Abhay Deol, who was equally unknown at the time, making his own debut alongside Ayesha Takia. It was, in every conventional sense, a small film, no stars, no established director, no obvious reason for anyone to pay attention.And then Sunny Deol got involved, in the most unexpected way possible.A Rainy Afternoon in Simla That Changed EverythingSunny Deol, action hero and screen presence of considerable authority, is Abhay Deol's cousin. When word reached him that Abhay was planning to make a film with some unknown director, Sunny called for Imtiaz. Before the meeting, well-meaning people tried to warn the young filmmaker about what he was walking into. The warnings were colourful. Sunny was an action hero. Sunny was the angry young man of Hindi cinema. The script Imtiaz was carrying had a scene where the hero gets slapped. What would happen, people asked, if Sunny took offence? Imtiaz had never met a film star before. He went to Simla anyway, because he wanted to make the film.Sunny was shooting there and told Imtiaz to come either late at night, early morning, or when it rained. Imtiaz waited for rain. When it came, he narrated the script. Sunny listened. And when the narration ended, the man who had been described to Imtiaz as temperamental, physically intimidating and potentially dangerous simply said yes.The experience broke something open for Imtiaz. He has since described Sunny as a very shy and very sensitive man, someone who did not ask where Imtiaz had come from or what experience he had. He just heard the story and decided to back it. Imtiaz has said Sunny holds a very important position in his life because he gave him his first film, a statement of genuine, uncomplicated gratitude that tells you something about the kind of filmmaker Imtiaz has always been, someone who remembers where he started.The Story Itself: Simple, But Not NaiveSocha Na Tha follows Viren Oberoi, played by Abhay Deol in a performance of quiet, authentic charm, and Aditi, played by Ayesha Takia. They are brought together by their families in the time-honoured tradition of arranged introductions, but Viren has a girlfriend of three years, Karen, and refuses to go through with the match. The families fall out. Aditi's aunt feels insulted. What should have ended there, however, does not.Viren and Aditi meet again by coincidence. They bond quickly, naturally, without the weight of expectation that their first meeting carried. He takes her to Goa to help him figure out whether Karen truly loves him. In Goa, something shifts. Karen notices it. Viren notices it. By the time he returns, he knows he has fallen in love with Aditi, but Karen's engagement has already been announced, the product of years of fighting against familial and religious resistance to their relationship.What follows is not a simple resolution. Aditi, aware of the hurt her aunt has already experienced, refuses to be the cause of more disruption. She chooses propriety over feeling. Viren, miserable, eventually tells Karen the truth about his feelings and moves on. On the day of Aditi's engagement to someone else, Karen, in a generous and quietly remarkable gesture, seeks Aditi out and tells her that Viren is wretched without her, and that she should not marry someone she does not love.Aditi elopes. The families, after the initial shock, come around. The film ends on a note of gentle, earned happiness.None of it is operatic. None of it reaches for the grandeur that Imtiaz's later work would embrace so fully. But the film's instincts about love, about the difference between what feels right on paper and what actually matters, and about the quiet dignity of choosing your own story even within the constraints of family and expectation, are unmistakably Imtiaz Ali.   https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5k0CDfpTb30The Songs Nobody Talks About EnoughThe music of Socha Na Tha deserves its own moment of appreciation. The title track, sung by Sandesh Shandilya and Sadhana Sargam, has a lightness and warmth that perfectly matches the film's emotional register. Yaara Rab, featuring Sonu Nigam and Sanjeevini, carries the ache of something half-understood and not yet named. Abhi Abhi, performed by Sunidhi Chauhan, Sonu Nigam and Kunal Ganjawala, has the easy, uncomplicated happiness of a feeling that has just been admitted to for the first time.In a Hindi film landscape that was still dominated by considerably louder and more bombastic soundtracks, Socha Na Tha's music felt like a quieter, more honest conversation about what romantic longing actually feels like.What Happened After ReleaseThe film did not perform well at the box office. It came and went without making a significant commercial impact, the fate of many good small films that arrive without the machinery of stars and marketing budgets behind them. But it was not forgotten. It became what films like this sometimes become, a cult classic for those who found it, a film that people recommend in hushed, proprietary tones, the way you share something you are glad not everyone has discovered yet.For millenials who watched it on television or pirated CDs in the mid-2000s, Socha Na Tha has a particular warmth, the warmth of a film that did not try too hard and did not have to. It was just a good story, told by someone who already knew how to find the emotional truth in ordinary human situations, even before he had developed the full vocabulary of his later, more ambitious work.Why It Matters NowWith Main Vaapas Aaunga reminding audiences of Imtiaz Ali's gift for emotional specificity and his ability to locate the universal inside the deeply personal, this feels like the right moment to look back at where the journey began. Not with Jab We Met's spectacular highway monologue, not with Rockstar's anguished, broken arc, not with Highway's raw confrontation with trauma and freedom, but with two young people in Goa, a borrowed girlfriend, a rainy afternoon in Simla where a nervous first-time director narrated a script to Sunny Deol, and a film that taught Imtiaz Ali, and anyone who has seen it, that sometimes the things we never thought we would feel are the ones that matter most.Socha Na Tha. Never had thought. And yet, here we are.

title: MBA faces crisis in AI era, demands urgent rethink from business schools
description: For a degree that teaches disruption, it is quite remarkable how little the traditional MBA model has changed in the 118 years since its inception at Harvard. In fact, one could argue that the MBA has done a remarkable job of avoiding disruption. Over these years, there have probably been only five major innovations—the introduction of the case method at Harvard in 1924, the launch of the executive MBA at Chicago Booth in 1943, the advent of the quantitative MBA in the 1950s, the birth of the one-year MBA at INSEAD in 1959, and the launch of the stackable online MBA in the 2010s.Apart from these, the MBA has remained remarkably resilient, seemingly immune to changes in the world around it. It has endured the dot-com bust, the global meltdown in 2008, and the global pandemic. At various points, commentators have sounded the death knell for the MBA, convinced that it is losing its sheen and value. Yet, it continues with a product that supporters argue has stood the test of time, while detractors dismiss it as being out of touch with reality.Today, we are once again at a crossroads as we confront AI and its impact on jobs. If I were to pose the question in the title of this essay to an MBA class, I would expect about 20% to say “Yes”, about 30% to say “No”, and 50% to sit on the fence with a typical “It depends” or “Maybe”. This essay is aimed at these “Maybes”. I argue that over the last 100-plus years, the MBA has survived change but has barely evolved. The time is ripe for a radical rethink.In India, the IIMs were founded in the 1960s, driven by the urge to create world-class management institutions. IIM Ahmedabad partnered with Harvard, and IIM Calcutta with MIT Sloan. The aim was to move away from the rigidities of the traditional university system and enable these institutions to pursue high-quality research and pedagogy.Sixty-plus years later, one could argue that while we have got the pedagogy right, we continue to lag behind global b-schools in creating new knowledge. This may have been acceptable when changes in the global environment were slow or predictable. That is no longer the case. AI has transformed the career landscape, accelerating the emergence of non-linear pathways.Also Read: Why MBA education in the age of AI must move beyond analysis for leadershipDisruptions abound—driven by entrepreneurs who challenge the status quo, question traditional business models, and take on well-established incumbents. India is at the forefront of these disruptions. Shouldn’t we be driving more research in this area? Shouldn’t we be creating new knowledge and disseminating it in the classroom? In fact, shouldn’t we be practicing what we teach and disrupting the way MBAs are designed and structured?Instead, the system has spawned innumerable PGDM and MBA programmes, most of them virtually identical except for placement outcomes, campus infrastructure, and brand name. We are becoming victims of the very rigidity we once sought to escape.To be sure, there is innovation at the margins. Most b-schools today include AI and analytics as core components of their curriculum. Courses on e-commerce, digital marketing, and data science are widely offered to ensure that graduates are tech-literate. Several institutions are also experimenting with pedagogical approaches such as simulations, flipped classrooms, and role-plays.Yet, the core architecture remains largely unchanged. There have been few attempts to challenge the traditional two-year format aimed at students with limited work experience. Even fewer have questioned the functional silos in which students specialise—finance, marketing, operations, OB/HR, and others. Governed by regulations and rankings, incentives are aligned towards consistency and incremental change.This creates a widening gap between what recruiters need from fresh graduates and what institutions are able to deliver.Also Read: AI readiness in Indian business schools beyond the signallingAs institutions around the world grapple with a commoditised offering, value-conscious students, and increasingly sceptical recruiters, there is a clear need for a more agile MBA—one that not only adapts to changing market needs but anticipates them. Institutions must adopt a growth mindset: challenging long-held assumptions, listening closely to recruiters and students, experimenting even at the risk of failure, and executing at a far greater pace and scale. AI presents an opportunity to make this shift.The widespread adoption of AI across organisations will require graduates who are skilled in using these tools. However, the ability to simply use AI is unlikely to create significant value. A study in the US by neuroscientist and entrepreneur Vivienne Ming found that students performed poorly on long-term prediction tasks without AI, with any LLM outperforming them.More strikingly, when students were allowed to use an LLM, over 90% simply copied responses, performing no better than the tool itself. However, about 10% demonstrated superior outcomes by thinking critically and engaging with the output.It is this ability—to think, reason, and solve ill-defined problems—that will endure. The market is ready for an agile MBA that challenges the status quo and builds career-ready skills for a future that will be increasingly shaped by AI.

title: Charli XCX on her “mini-break” in Paris with Madonna: “I found it very inspiring to hang out with her”
description:
The two singers ended speculation over a possible feud by attending a fashion show together last week
The post Charli XCX on her “mini-break” in Paris with Madonna: “I found it very inspiring to hang out with her” appeared first on NME.
content:
Charli XCX has opened up about her relationship with Madonna, after the pair appeared to end speculation of a feud by partying together in Paris.

READ MORE: The Big Read – Madonna: ”People pick on me. That’s just the way it is”

Charli’s recent single ‘Rock Music’, taken from her upcoming album ‘Music, Fashion, Film’, caused debate over its bold declaration: “I think the dancefloor is dead, so now we’re making rock music”.
The lyric arrived as Madonna was gearing up to release ‘Confessions II’, the long-anticipated sequel to 2005’s ‘Confessions On A Dance Floor’. She later appeared to respond on Instagram, writing: “If your Dance floor feels dead, maybe you’re playing the wrong music.”
However, the pair were spotted together in Paris in the last week, bringing an apparent end to speculation of tension between the two pop stars.
Now, Charli has given an interview to Nick Grimshaw on BBC 6 Music, where she discussed her recent “mini-break” with Madonna.
“Me and Madonna had a mini break together, essentially is what happened. We went to Paris and we just couldn’t stop hanging out with each other. And it was fab!” Charli said.

  










View this post on Instagram

























“We’ve met before in the past but I don’t know that she knows that – but we were put together on a group text just a couple of weeks ago, which is obviously insane.”
Charli went on to explain that the pair then ended up throwing parties together in the French capital.
“So we did – but then we actually accidentally threw two parties,” she said. “She’s very like… I mean, she’s so cool. And she is so interested in really talking about the music that she makes, and how and why, and I found it very inspiring to hang out with her.”
Charli also admitted that she was not necessarily a Madonna fan when she was younger, but came to appreciate her work more deeply as she grew older.
“I think when I became like a bit more interested in how music is art and artful – I think that’s when I began my Madonna time,” she said. “My first big song that I fell in love with of hers was ‘Hollywood’. That song really hit me in a way.”
The two artists were seated next to one another at the Saint Laurent menswear spring/summer 2027 fashion show on June 23, and were later seen smoking and chatting with other stars in attendance, including actors Connor Storrie and Debi Mazar.
At the end of the night, the two danced together to Madonna’s hits, like ‘Thief of Hearts’ and ‘Hung Up’, from behind the DJ booth at the after-party.

  










View this post on Instagram

























Charli has since clarified that ‘Rock Music’ was not intended as a literal dismissal of dance music. “That lyric is very much about my relationship with ‘Brat’, and my personal experience with that album,” she explained. “Dance music is in an incredible place.”
She also pushed back on the idea that her next album is a straightforward rock record, saying: “I know that there’s been a lot of conversation around me making a rock album, which is something that I never said.”
‘Music, Fashion, Film’ is set for release on July 24, and features a black-and-white cover shot by Aidan Zamiri with John Cale, Marc Jacobs and Martin Scorsese. You can pre-order it here.
Elsewhere, Charli recently spoke candidly about her mental health, saying: “I’m in the worst place mentally that I’ve been in my life.”
She added that she was “struggling” in the wake of the ‘Brat’ era, while also moving into a new creative chapter across music, fashion and film.
She will return to the stage to make her headline debut at Reading & Leeds in August, topping the festival bill alongside Fontaines D.C., Raye, Florence + The Machine, Dave and Chase & Status. Visit here for tickets.
Madonna’s ‘Confessions II’, meanwhile, will be out on July 3 and you can pre-order it here.
The post Charli XCX on her “mini-break” in Paris with Madonna: “I found it very inspiring to hang out with her” appeared first on NME.

title: Ars Live: What's the latest in the aftermath of the New Glenn catastrophe?
description: Join us on the livestream at 1 pm ET and ask questions about the aftermath of New Glenn.
content:
Nearly a month has passed since the New Glenn rocket exploded on its launch pad in Florida, creating a massive fireball. It was likely the largest ever rocket explosion at the historic Florida spaceport, and we are still dealing with its implications today.
The rocket's explosion took out its only launch pad, LC-36A. So even if Blue Origin can quickly diagnose the cause of the failure, it has nowhere to launch the New Glenn rocket from. Company officials, including founder Jeff Bezos, have said the vehicle will return to flight at LC-36A before the end of this year, though there is widespread skepticism about that timeline.
Meanwhile, we have more questions than answers about a rocket that had become increasingly central to the needs of NASA and commercial customers. What does this failure mean for the Artemis Program to land humans on the Moon? What do we know about the timing of Artemis III and the lunar landing mission, Artemis IV? What about the Moon base?Read full article

title: त्र्यंबकेश्वरला अमृतकुंडाच्या तळाशी शिवपिंडीचे दर्शन; पुरातत्त्व विभागाच्या स्वच्छता मोहिमेत उलगडले दुर्मिळ दृश्य
description: त्र्यंबकेश्वर | वार्ताहर Trimbakeshwar आद्य ज्योतिर्लिंग श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर महादेव मंदिर परिसरातील ऐतिहासिक अमृतकुंडाची भारतीय पुरातत्त्व विभागामार्फत स्वच्छता करण्यात आल्यानंतर कुंडाच्या तळाशी असलेल्या शिवपिंडीचे दर्शन घडले. अनेक वर्षांनंतर समोर आलेल्या या दुर्मिळ दृश्यामुळे उपस्थितांमध्ये उत्सुकता निर्माण झाली. संवर्धनाच्या कामाचा भाग म्हणून भारतीय पुरातत्त्व विभागाने अमृतकुंडातील संपूर्ण पाणी उपसून कुंडाची स्वच्छता केली. त्यानंतर तळाशी असलेले छोटे शिवलिंग […]
content:
त्र्यंबकेश्वर | वार्ताहर Trimbakeshwar



आद्य ज्योतिर्लिंग श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर महादेव मंदिर परिसरातील ऐतिहासिक अमृतकुंडाची भारतीय पुरातत्त्व विभागामार्फत स्वच्छता करण्यात आल्यानंतर कुंडाच्या तळाशी असलेल्या शिवपिंडीचे दर्शन घडले. अनेक वर्षांनंतर समोर आलेल्या या दुर्मिळ दृश्यामुळे उपस्थितांमध्ये उत्सुकता निर्माण झाली.



संवर्धनाच्या कामाचा भाग म्हणून भारतीय पुरातत्त्व विभागाने अमृतकुंडातील संपूर्ण पाणी उपसून कुंडाची स्वच्छता केली. त्यानंतर तळाशी असलेले छोटे शिवलिंग स्पष्टपणे दिसून आले. यावेळी भारतीय पुरातत्त्व विभागाचे अधिकारी कृष्णमूर्ती व त्यांचे सहकारी तसेच श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर देवस्थान ट्रस्टचे विश्वस्त कैलास घुले उपस्थित होते.



इतिहासाच्या दृष्टीने महत्त्व असलेले अमृतकुंड हे पेशवेकाळात श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर मंदिराच्या जीर्णोद्धारावेळी दगडी बांधकामातून उभारण्यात आले असल्याचे सांगितले जाते. सुमारे ६५ फूट खोल असलेल्या या कुंडातील पाणी भगवान त्र्यंबकेश्वरांच्या नित्यपूजा व अभिषेकासाठी वापरले जाते. कुंडाची रचना अत्यंत कलात्मक असून त्यामध्ये उतरता यावे यासाठी दगडी पायऱ्यांचीही व्यवस्था करण्यात आली आहे.



सुरक्षेच्या कारणास्तव अमृतकुंडात भाविकांना प्रवेश दिला जात नाही. प्राचीन वास्तुशास्त्रानुसार मंदिर जितके उंच, तितक्याच खोलीचे तीर्थकुंड असावे, अशी संकल्पना मानली जाते. श्री त्र्यंबकेश्वर मंदिराची उंची सुमारे ६५ फूट असल्याने त्याच प्रमाणात अमृतकुंडाची खोली ठेवण्यात आल्याचे मानले जाते.



दरवर्षी पावसाळ्यात अमृतकुंड पाण्याने भरते. विशेषतः मुसळधार पाऊस झाल्यास हे कुंड पूर्ण क्षमतेने भरत असून त्यातील पाणी वर्षभर धार्मिक विधींसाठी उपयोगात आणले जाते.

title: Beyond the Ayodhya Chanda Chori Scam: The Likely Political Fallout | Opinion
description: All 8 arrested in the Ram temple donation row have been sent to police custody till June 29 and Rs 79.85 lakh has been recovered, according to officials.
content: With the alleged donation scam in Ayodhya coming to light, the Opposition is gunning for Champat Rai, Ram Janmbhoomi trust general secretary, who has now resigned on "moral grounds", saying don’t let the big fish get away. Champat Rai, has been for long associated with the Ram Janmbhoomi movement as a close aide of VHP leader Ashok Singhal. With his name cropping up now and political parties, demanding accountability, swift action in the form of an SIT report, an FIR being filed and now arrests being conducted are rapidly playing out. Among the arrested accused is a former close aide of Champat Rai, Tinnu Yadav. Therefore, the role of the trust members will obviously have to be investigated. Whether it’s about acts of omission or commission, Direct or indirect involvement, lack of adequate oversight mechanisms or simply the moral responsibility of being the custodian of the trust as its general secretary. However, if that happens, it opens up the question of what bearing it will have upon private individuals in the trust. In this case, it is representatives of the Sangh who are appointed as members of the trust, apart from Government officers and spiritual leaders. With this act of alleged corruption sending shockwaves across the country, does it now pave the way for a change in the way the government appointed but independently functioning temple trust operates? The irony is not to be missed. The RSS has been supportive of movements to free temples from government control particularly in the Southern part of India. Will the alleged scam now lead to an increased government control or oversight over the trust then becomes an important question? The issue of government control also has a bearing on how opposition will target the ruling party in the upcoming Uttar Pradesh assembly elections next year. In Southern states like Kerala where improper state government handling of temple affairs in Sabarimala - ie. scams, pilferage and general insensitivity at the hill temple - are believed to be among the contributing factors towards the exit of the LDF government. In Uttar Pradesh can decisive action by the police, prevent such an eventuality from happening? There are two factors at play here. One, the temple is not directly under the control of the state government unlike in Kerala. Secondly, the Chief Minister’s own track record in governance, Law & Order and his Personal image is by many accounts likely to override such issues. Therefore a swift probe and speedy trial might help dissipate atleast some of the anger by the time polls arrive.However, for devotees who at the time of the Pran Pratishtha were told that this was a pivotal movement in a 500 year long, civilisational battle, they would obviously be upset at the way things have unfolded within less than two years. They have certainly been shocked and stunned with the sensational charges of corruption given that several prominent individuals have come out to say that they recieved no receipt while donating jewellery or money to the trust. They are now wondering about the fate of their donations. How quickly then, can the government restore their faith through speedy justice and transparent management mechanisms becomes a very important factor. Especially so, since these devotees who have rallied behind the Ram Janmabhoomi movement played a pivotal role in the rise of BJP over the last three and a half decades.
